channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Cloudflare Workers AI Issues
item.description: Mar 26, 04:26 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 26, 04:16 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 26, 03:03 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare has identified an issue whereby customers might experience errors from inference requests to...

item.title: Understanding LLM Performance Degradation in Multi-Instance Processing: The Roles of Instance Count and Context Length
item.description: arXiv:2603.22608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Users often rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) for processing multiple documents or performing analysis over a number of instances. For example, analysing the overall sentiment of a number of movie reviews requires an LLM to process the sentiment of...

item.title: Bridging the Know-Act Gap via Task-Level Autoregressive Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.22619v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs often generate seemingly valid answers to flawed or ill-posed inputs. This is not due to missing knowledge: under discriminative prompting, the same models can mostly identify such issues, yet fail to reflect this in standard generative...

item.title: Graph-Aware Late Chunking for Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Biomedical Literature
item.description: arXiv:2603.22633v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for biomedical literature are typically evaluated using ranking metrics like Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), which measure how well the system identifies the single most relevant chunk. We argue that for...

item.title: Benchmarking Multi-Agent LLM Architectures for Financial Document Processing: A Comparative Study of Orchestration Patterns, Cost-Accuracy Tradeoffs and Production Scaling Strategies
item.description: arXiv:2603.22651v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The adoption of large language models (LLMs) for structured information extraction from financial documents has accelerated rapidly, yet production deployments face fundamental architectural decisions with limited empirical guidance. We present a...

item.title: MuQ-Eval: An Open-Source Per-Sample Quality Metric for AI Music Generation Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.22677v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Distributional metrics such as Fr\'echet Audio Distance cannot score individual music clips and correlate poorly with human judgments, while the only per-sample learned metric achieving high human correlation is closed-source. We introduce MUQ-EVAL,...

item.title: Beyond Accuracy: Introducing a Symbolic-Mechanistic Approach to Interpretable Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accuracy-based evaluation cannot reliably distinguish genuine generalization from shortcuts like memorization, leakage, or brittle heuristics, especially in small-data regimes. In this position paper, we argue for mechanism-aware evaluation that...

item.title: Implicit Turn-Wise Policy Optimization for Proactive User-LLM Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2603.23550v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-turn human-AI collaboration is fundamental to deploying interactive services such as adaptive tutoring, conversational recommendation, and professional consultation. However, optimizing these interactions via reinforcement learning is hindered...

item.title: Upper Entropy for 2-Monotone Lower Probabilities
item.description: arXiv:2603.23558v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Uncertainty quantification is a key aspect in many tasks such as model selection/regularization, or quantifying prediction uncertainties to perform active learning or OOD detection. Within credal approaches that consider modeling uncertainty as...

item.title: Synthetic Mixed Training: Scaling Parametric Knowledge Acquisition Beyond RAG
item.description: arXiv:2603.23562v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthetic data augmentation helps language models learn new knowledge in data-constrained domains. However, naively scaling existing synthetic data methods by training on more synthetic tokens or using stronger generators yields diminishing returns...

item.title: Safe Reinforcement Learning with Preference-based Constraint Inference
item.description: arXiv:2603.23565v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safe reinforcement learning (RL) is a standard paradigm for safety-critical decision making. However, real-world safety constraints can be complex, subjective, and even hard to explicitly specify. Existing works on constraint inference rely on...

item.title: Leveraging Computerized Adaptive Testing for Cost-effective Evaluation of Large Language Models in Medical Benchmarking
item.description: arXiv:2603.23506v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid proliferation of large language models (LLMs) in healthcare creates an urgent need for scalable and psychometrically sound evaluation methods. Conventional static benchmarks are costly to administer repeatedly, vulnerable to data...

item.title: Beyond Masks: Efficient, Flexible Diffusion Language Models via Deletion-Insertion Processes
item.description: arXiv:2603.23507v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) relying on token masking and unmasking have shown promise in language modeling, their computational efficiency and generation flexibility remain constrained by the masking paradigm. In this paper, we...

item.title: Fast and Faithful: Real-Time Verification for Long-Document Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.23508v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is increasingly deployed in enterprise search and document-centric assistants, where responses must be grounded in long and complex source materials. In practice, verifying that generated answers faithfully reflect...

item.title: Internal Safety Collapse in Frontier Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.23509v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work identifies a critical failure mode in frontier large language models (LLMs), which we term Internal Safety Collapse (ISC): under certain task conditions, models enter a state in which they continuously generate harmful content while...

item.title: Visuospatial Perspective Taking in Multimodal Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.23510v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As multimodal language models (MLMs) are increasingly used in social and collaborative settings, it is crucial to evaluate their perspective-taking abilities. Existing benchmarks largely rely on text-based vignettes or static scene understanding,...

item.title: [AINews] The Biggest Claude Launch of All Time
item.description: We thought about this carefully before choosing hyperbole; but it is warranted.

item.title: XPeng Unveils 2026 MONA M03 with Turing AI Chip and Enhanced Range
item.description: XPeng has launched the 2026 MONA M03, featuring the new Turing AI chip with 750 TOPS of computing power and a maximum driving range extended to 640 kilometers.

item.title: Kuaishou Expects $3.6 Billion Capex in 2026 to Boost AI Infrastructure
item.description: Kuaishou Technology expects its 2026 capital expenditure to reach approximately $3.6 billion, with the significant increase primarily funding computing infrastructure for its Kling large model.

item.title: XtalPi Turns Profitable in 2025 as Revenue Surges 201%
item.description: XtalPi Holdings reported its first annual profit in 2025, with revenue surging 201% to approximately $112 million, making it the first profitable publicly listed AI for Science company in Hong Kong.

item.title: SUNRISING AI Raises Over $14 Million Within Six Months of Founding
item.description: Industrial embodied AI startup SUNRISING AI has raised over $14 million in multiple early-stage rounds within six months of its founding, with IDG Capital among the co-leads.

item.title: Kunlun Tech’s Mureka V8 Tops Global AI Music Model Rankings
item.description: Chinese tech firm Kunlun Tech’s AI music model Mureka V8 has claimed the top spot in both vocal and instrumental categories on the Artificial Analysis benchmark, surpassing global rivals like Suno V4.5 and Udio v1.5 Allegro.

item.title: Access for Infrastructure RDP intermittent connectivity issues
item.description: Mar 26, 01:20 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 25, 22:02 UTCInvestigating - Customers may experience transient issues using Access Browser-based RDP

item.title: Container Logs issues
item.description: Mar 25, 23:00 UTCResolved - Cloudflare has identified an issue impacting Containers Logs, customers may have seen a reduced level of logs between March 25th 15:50 UTC to 22:55 UTCThis incident has now been resolved and logs are now flowing in correctly.

item.title: LeakBase Admin Arrested in Russia Over Massive Stolen Credential Marketplace
item.description: The alleged administrator of the LeakBase cybercrime forum has been arrested by Russian law enforcement authorities, state media reported Thursday. According to TASS and MVD Media, a news website linked to the Russian Interior Ministry, the suspect is a resident of the city of Taganrog. The suspect...

item.title: Roundtables: The Next Era of Space Exploration
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Whether it’s the race to find life on Mars, the campaign to outsmart killer asteroids, or the quest to make the moon a permanent home to astronauts, scientists’ efforts in space can tell us more about where humanity is headed. This subscriber-only...

item.title: Elevated connection reset errors in Cowork
item.description: Mar 25, 16:56 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to investigate connection errors which occur during Claude Cowork sessions. This issue can be resolved by restarting the Claude Desktop application.Mar 25, 14:33 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 25, 16:58 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.Mar 25, 15:37 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 25, 15:04 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Missing PII information display for Cloudflare One Super Admins
item.description: Mar 25, 16:37 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 25, 16:13 UTCInvestigating - The Cloudflare One dashboard is not showing PII information to Super Admins on Logging side panel details.

item.title: Lyria 3 Pro: Create longer tracks in more
item.description: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro, which unlocks longer tracks with structural awareness. We’re also bringing Lyria to more Google products and surfaces.

item.title: Onit Security Raises $11 Million for Exposure Management Platform
item.description: The startup will invest in product development and go-to-market efforts as it expands into new sectors. The post Onit Security Raises $11 Million for Exposure Management Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated Errors on claude.ai
item.description: Mar 25, 15:43 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 25, 15:27 UTCMonitoring - We are currently seeing recovery, and our engineers are working to ensure no further recurrences. We will provide an update as soon as possible.Mar 25, 14:33 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a...

item.title: Why this battery company is pivoting to AI
item.description: Qichao Hu doesn’t mince words about how he sees the state of the battery industry. “Almost every Western battery company has either died or is going to die. It’s kind of the reality,” he says. Hu is the CEO of SES AI, a Massachusetts-based battery company. It once had aims of making huge amounts...

item.title: Russian Cybercriminal Gets 2-Year Prison Sentence in US
item.description: Ilya Angelov was a member of the cybercrime group tracked as TA-551, Shathak, Gold Cabin, Monster Libra, and ATK236. The post Russian Cybercriminal Gets 2-Year Prison Sentence in US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Uses Solana Dead Drops to Deliver RAT and Steal Browser, Crypto Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new evolution of the GlassWorm campaign that delivers a multi-stage framework capable of comprehensive data theft and installing a remote access trojan (RAT), which deploys an information-stealing Google Chrome extension masquerading as an offline version of...

item.title: AI Speeds Attacks, But Identity Remains Cybersecurity’s Weakest Link
item.description: PwC finds AI is amplifying speed and scale of attacks, as identity theft evolves into a cybercriminal supply chain. The post AI Speeds Attacks, But Identity Remains Cybersecurity’s Weakest Link appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: This startup wants to change how mathematicians do math
item.description: Axiom Math, a startup based in Palo Alto, California, has released a free new AI tool for mathematicians, designed to discover mathematical patterns that could unlock solutions to long-standing problems. The tool, called Axplorer, is a redesign of an existing one called PatternBoost that François...

item.title: iOS, macOS 26.4 Roll Out With Fresh Security Patches
item.description: Apple released security fixes for older devices as well, in iOS 18.7.7, iPadOS 18.7.7, macOS Sequoia 15.7.5, and macOS Sonoma 14.8.5. The post iOS, macOS 26.4 Roll Out With Fresh Security Patches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 25, 13:10 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 25, 10:06 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 25, 09:35 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Warsaw, Poland (WAW)
item.description: Mar 25, 12:53 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 25, 12:43 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 25, 12:29 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with network performance in Warsaw, Poland (WAW)....

item.title: The Download: reawakening frozen brains, and the AI Hype Index returns
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain  L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits in a vat at a storage...

item.title: FCC Bans New Routers Made Outside the US Over National Security Risks
item.description: The ban aligns with a White House determination that all routers produced abroad are a threat to national security. The post FCC Bans New Routers Made Outside the US Over National Security Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Vulnerabilities affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Citrix NetScaler Gateway
item.description: UK organisations encouraged to take immediate action to mitigate two recently disclosed vulnerabilities affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Citrix NetScaler Gateway.

item.title: The Kill Chain Is Obsolete When Your AI Agent Is the Threat
item.description: In September 2025, Anthropic disclosed that a state-sponsored threat actor used an AI coding agent to execute an autonomous cyber espionage campaign against 30 global targets. The AI handled 80-90% of tactical operations on its own, performing reconnaissance, writing exploit code, and attempting...

item.title: Russian Hacker Sentenced to 2 Years for TA551 Botnet-Driven Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) said a Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison for managing a botnet that was used to launch ransomware attacks against U.S. companies. Ilya Angelov, 40, of Tolyatti, Russia, was also fined $100,000. Angelov, who went by the online aliases...

item.title: Agentic commerce runs on truth and context
item.description: Imagine telling a digital agent, “Use my points and book a family trip to Italy. Keep it within budget, pick hotels we’ve liked before, and handle the details.” Instead of returning a list of links, the agent assembles an itinerary and executes the purchase. That shift, from assistance to...

item.title: Device Code Phishing Hits 340+ Microsoft 365 Orgs Across Five Countries via OAuth Abuse
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to an active device code phishing campaign that's targeting Microsoft 365 identities across more than 340 organizations in the U.S., Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and Germany. The activity, per Huntress, was first spotted on February 19, 2026, with...

item.title: [AINews] Apple's War on Slop
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on the End of Sora, LiteLLM, AI2, and other not so happy news.

item.title: Unable to download or preview project files
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance) Files (Operational)

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Mar 24, 20:38 UTCUpdate - We are investigating elevated error rates affecting multiple GitHub services including Actions, Issues, Pull Requests, Webhooks, Codespaces, and login functionality. Some users may have experienced errors when accessing these features. Most services are now showing signs...
